 {"id":"caam-j6rg","title":"Implement Claude credentials identity parser","description":"# Task: Implement Claude credentials identity parser

## Overview
Extract identity information from Claude Code credential files (.credentials.json) without API calls.

## Background
Claude stores identity differently from Codex. The .credentials.json file contains a claudeAiOauth object with:
- accountId
- subscriptionType: "max", "pro", etc.
- May contain email in some versions

## Technical Details

### Function
```go
// ExtractFromClaudeCredentials reads Claude .credentials.json and extracts identity
func ExtractFromClaudeCredentials(path string) (*Identity, error) {
    // 1. Read .credentials.json
    // 2. Parse JSON structure
    // 3. Extract claudeAiOauth object
    // 4. Map to Identity struct
}
```

### Claude Credentials Structure (observed)
```json
{
  "claudeAiOauth": {
    "accessToken": "...",
    "refreshToken": "...",
    "expiresAt": 1234567890,
    "accountId": "abc123",
    "subscriptionType": "max"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly parses Claude credentials format
- [ ] Extracts accountId and subscriptionType
- [ ] Handles missing fields gracefully
- [ ] Works with different Claude credential versions
- [ ] Sub-millisecond extraction time

## Test Cases
1. Parse valid credentials with all fields
2. Parse credentials with minimal fields
3. Handle corrupted JSON file
4. Handle missing file

## Files to Create/Modify
- internal/identity/claude.go
- internal/identity/claude_test.go

 {"id":"caam-j7pp","title":"Implement caam cost command","description":"# Task: Implement caam cost command

## Overview
Create the caam cost command that displays token cost analysis, subscription value comparison, and usage trends.

## Command Interface
```
$ caam cost [provider] [flags]

Flags:
  --last duration    Time period to analyze (e.g., "7d", "30d", "24h")
  --format string    Output format: table (default), json, csv
  --profile string   Specific profile to analyze (default: all)
```

## Output Example
```
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    TOKEN COST ANALYSIS (Last 30 Days)                  ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  SUMMARY                                                                ║
║  Total tokens:     8,234,567                                           ║
║    Input:          2,456,789 (30%)                                     ║
║    Output:         4,567,890 (55%)                                     ║
║    Cache read:     987,654 (12%)                                       ║
║    Cache create:   222,234 (3%)                                        ║
║                                                                         ║
║  MODEL BREAKDOWN                                                        ║
║  claude-3-opus     ████████████████░░░░ 78%   6,423,000 tokens  $189.23║
║  claude-3-sonnet   ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 19%   1,564,567 tokens   $23.47║
║                                                                         ║
║  COST COMPARISON                                                        ║
║  If pay-per-token:  $213.01                                            ║
║  Your subscription: $200.00/month                                       ║
║  Savings this month: $13.01 (6%)                                       ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

## Technical Details

### Command Implementation
```go
var costCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "cost [provider]",
    Short: "Analyze token costs and subscription value",
    RunE: runCost,
}

func runCost(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // 1. Parse time period flag
    // 2. Scan logs for the period
    // 3. Aggregate by model
    // 4. Calculate costs using pricing tables
    // 5. Compare to subscription cost
    // 6. Render output
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows token breakdown by type (input/output/cache)
- [ ] Shows model breakdown with percentages
- [ ] Calculates accurate API-equivalent costs
- [ ] Compares to subscription cost when known
- [ ] Supports table, JSON, and CSV output
- [ ] Handles empty log directories gracefully

## Files to Create
- cmd/caam/cmd/cost.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/cost_test.go

## Overview
Create functions to aggregate LogEntry data into daily/weekly/monthly summaries with model breakdowns.

## Technical Details

### Aggregation Functions
```go
// Aggregate aggregates log entries into a TokenUsage summary
func Aggregate(entries []*LogEntry) *TokenUsage

// AggregateByModel groups entries by model
func AggregateByModel(entries []*LogEntry) map[string]*ModelTokenUsage
```

### DailyUsage Structure
```go
type DailyUsage struct {
    Date     string       // "2025-01-10"

    ByModel  map[string]*ModelTokenUsage
}
```

 {"id":"caam-je9o","title":"E2E: Monitor and precheck commands with all output formats","description":"# Task: E2E Tests for Monitor and Precheck Commands

## Overview
End-to-end tests for the caam monitor and caam precheck commands with all output formats.

## Test Categories

### 1. Monitor Command Tests

#### Table Format
```go
func TestMonitorTableFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format table --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "table", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output length: %d bytes", len(output))
    t.Logf("[TEST] First 500 chars:\n%s", output[:min(500, len(output))])
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Contains(t, output, "LIVE USAGE MONITOR")
    require.Contains(t, output, "CLAUDE")
}
```

#### Brief Format (tmux integration)
```go
func TestMonitorBriefFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format brief --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "brief", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output: %q", output)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Length: %d chars", len(output))
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Less(t, len(output), 80, "Brief format should fit in status bar")
    require.Regexp(t, `\w+:\d+%`, output) // e.g., "claude:42%"
}
```

#### JSON Format
```go
func TestMonitorJSONFormat(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam monitor --format json --once")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "monitor", "--format", "json", "--once")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] JSON output:\n%s", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    var result map[string]interface{}
    err = json.Unmarshal([]byte(output), &result)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Parse result: err=%v", err)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    require.Contains(t, result, "updated_at")
    require.Contains(t, result, "profiles")
}
```

### 2. Precheck Command Tests

#### Full Precheck Output
```go
func TestPrecheckFull(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Output:\n%s", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    require.Contains(t, output, "SESSION PLANNER")
    require.Contains(t, output, "RECOMMENDED")
}
```

#### Brief Format
```go
func TestPrecheckBrief(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude --format brief")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude", "--format", "brief")
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Brief output: %q", output)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    // Should be a one-liner recommendation
}
```

#### JSON Format
```go
func TestPrecheckJSON(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Running: caam precheck claude --format json")
    
    output, err := runCommand("caam", "precheck", "claude", "--format", "json")
    
    var result map[string]interface{}
    json.Unmarshal([]byte(output), &result)
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Keys: %v", keys(result))
    
    require.Contains(t, result, "recommended")
    require.Contains(t, result, "backups")
    require.Contains(t, result, "forecast")
}
```

### 3. Error Handling Tests
- No profiles configured
- Provider not supported
- Network errors (mock)
- Partial failures (some profiles error)

### 4. Integration Tests
- Monitor + jq piping
- Precheck + shell scripting
- Output to file

## Test Infrastructure

### Mock Server for Rate Limits
```go
func setupMockRateLimitServer(t *testing.T) *httptest.Server {
    return httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        t.Logf("[TEST] Mock request: %s %s", r.Method, r.URL.Path)
        // Return mock rate limit data
    }))
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log exact command being run
- Log full stdout/stderr
- Log exit code
- Log any parsing errors
- Log timing of operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All output formats work correctly
- [ ] Monitor --once exits cleanly
- [ ] Precheck shows all sections
- [ ] JSON output is valid and parseable
- [ ] Brief output fits in 80 chars
- [ ] Errors are handled gracefully
- [ ] Tests work with mock data

## Files to Create
- cmd/caam/cmd/monitor_e2e_test.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/precheck_e2e_test.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/testutil_test.go

 {"id":"caam-jig","title":"Headless Authentication Support (Device Code Flow)","description":"# Headless Server Support

## Status: Core Features Complete!

The primary headless workflows are now fully functional:

### ✅ Completed

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Device auth setup | Works via native \`codex login --device-auth\` + \`caam backup\` |
| Hit-limit → switch → resume | Works via \`caam activate\` + native \`codex resume\` |
| Vault transfer | ✅ \`caam export/import\` implemented (caam-zha) |
| Documentation | ✅ AGENTS.md updated (caam-1r6) |
| AuthModeDeviceCode constant | ✅ Added (caam-hzu) |

### Remaining (P2/P3 - Profile Isolation Mode Only)

These are for the **advanced** Profile Isolation mode, not the primary workflow:

| ID | Task | Priority | Purpose |
|----|------|----------|---------|
| caam-v89 | --device-code CLI flag | P2 | For \`caam login\` in Profile Isolation mode |
| caam-ela | Codex provider support | P2 | Implementation for above |
| caam-0gy | Session resume | P3 | For Profile Isolation mode only |

## The Working Workflow

```bash
# SETUP (on headless server)
codex login --device-auth    # Native Codex
caam backup codex account1   # Existing caam

# HIT LIMIT → SWITCH → RESUME
caam activate codex account2             # < 1 second!
codex resume 019b2e3d-... "proceed"      # Native Codex
```

## Closed Tasks

| ID | Reason |
|----|--------|
| caam-1xv | Research complete - Claude lacks good headless option |
| caam-ai6 | Research complete - vault transfer is best for Gemini |
| caam-e79 | Removed - headless detection was over-engineering |
| caam-m5a | Removed - QR code was gold-plating |","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:36:00.94446079-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:40:21.386555184-05:00","close_reason":"Core headless workflows complete: device auth setup, hit-limit→switch→resume, vault transfer, and documentation. Remaining tasks (caam-v89, caam-ela) are P2 enhancements for Profile Isolation mode."}
 {"id":"caam-jjoi","title":"Create internal/monitor package with state management","description":"# Task: Create internal/monitor package with state management

## Overview
Create the core monitor package that manages state for real-time usage monitoring across all profiles.

## Technical Details

### State Structures
```go
// internal/monitor/monitor.go

type Monitor struct {
    interval    time.Duration
    providers   []string
    
    fetcher     *usage.MultiProfileFetcher
    vault       *authfile.Vault
    healthStore *health.Storage
    db          *db.DB
    
    state       *MonitorState
    mu          sync.RWMutex
}

type MonitorState struct {
    Profiles  map[string]*ProfileState  // "provider/name" -> state
    UpdatedAt time.Time
    Errors    []string
}

type ProfileState struct {
    Provider      string
    ProfileName   string
    Usage         *usage.UsageInfo
    Health        health.HealthStatus
    PoolStatus    authpool.PoolStatus
    InCooldown    bool
    CooldownUntil *time.Time
    Alert         *Alert
}

type Alert struct {
    Type    AlertType
    Message string
    Since   time.Time
}

type AlertType int
const (
    AlertNone AlertType = iota
    AlertWarning    // 70-85%
    AlertCritical   // 85-95%
    AlertExhausted  // 95-100%
)
```

### Core Methods
```go
func NewMonitor(opts ...MonitorOption) *Monitor
func (m *Monitor) Start(ctx context.Context) error  // Blocking loop
func (m *Monitor) Refresh(ctx context.Context) error  // One-shot refresh
func (m *Monitor) GetState() *MonitorState
func (m *Monitor) GetProfile(provider, name string) *ProfileState
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Thread-safe state management
- [ ] Concurrent fetching across all profiles
- [ ] Proper error handling (partial failures dont break monitor)
- [ ] Efficient memory usage for long-running sessions
- [ ] Configurable refresh intervals

## Files to Create
- internal/monitor/monitor.go
- internal/monitor/state.go
- internal/monitor/alerts.go
- internal/monitor/monitor_test.go

 {"id":"caam-kf31","title":"Implement rate limit detection callback in runner","description":"# Task: Implement rate limit detection callback in runner

## Overview
Enhance the exec.Runner to detect rate limits in real-time output and trigger a callback for handling.

## Background
The current caam run command uses internal/ratelimit/detector.go to check for rate limit patterns. We need to integrate this with the PTY controller to enable real-time detection during execution.

## Technical Details

### Enhanced Runner Options
```go
// internal/exec/runner.go

type RunOptions struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    // NEW: Smart handoff options
    OnRateLimit     func(ctx context.Context) error  // Callback when rate limit detected
    RateLimitDelay  time.Duration                     // Debounce time before triggering
}
```

### Output Monitoring
```go
func (r *Runner) monitorOutput(ctx context.Context, reader io.Reader) {
    scanner := bufio.NewScanner(reader)
    for scanner.Scan() {
        line := scanner.Text()
        
        // Pass through to actual stdout
        fmt.Println(line)
        
        // Check for rate limit
        if r.detector.Check(line) {
            if r.opts.OnRateLimit \!= nil {
                go r.opts.OnRateLimit(ctx)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Dependencies
- Create PTY controller for command injection

## Files to Modify
- internal/exec/runner.go
- internal/exec/options.go (if exists)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:18:56.750001601-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:29:44.38181202-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:29:44.38181202-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented rate limit detection callback in exec.Runner","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-kf31","depends_on_id":"caam-iz4s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:20:38.33001028-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"caam-l3w7","title":"Unit tests for internal/authpool package","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for internal/authpool Package

## Overview
Comprehensive unit tests for the AuthPool manager, token monitor loop, and profile state management.

## Test Categories

### 1. Pool Initialization Tests
```go
func TestPoolCreation(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Creating empty pool")
    pool := authpool.NewAuthPool(vault)
    t.Logf("[TEST] Pool created with %d profiles", pool.Count())
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Adding profile")
    err := pool.AddProfile("claude", "alice@gmail.com")
    t.Logf("[TEST] Add result: err=%v", err)
}
```

### 2. Token Expiry Detection Tests
- Token expires in future (should be Ready)
- Token expires soon (should trigger refresh)
- Token already expired (should be Expired)
- Token with no expiry info (should be Unknown)

### 3. Refresh Logic Tests
```go
func TestRefreshTrigger(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("[TEST] Setting up profile with 10min TTL")
    profile := &PooledProfile{
        TokenExpiry: time.Now().Add(10 * time.Minute),
    }
    
    pool := NewAuthPool(WithRefreshThreshold(15 * time.Minute))
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Checking if refresh needed")
    needsRefresh := pool.NeedsRefresh(profile)
    t.Logf("[TEST] TTL=10min, Threshold=15min, NeedsRefresh=%v", needsRefresh)
    
    assert.True(t, needsRefresh) // 10min < 15min threshold
}
```

### 4. Concurrent Access Tests
- Multiple goroutines reading state
- Concurrent refresh triggers
- Race condition detection with -race flag

### 5. Monitor Loop Tests
- Loop starts and stops cleanly
- Refresh triggers at correct intervals
- Context cancellation stops loop
- Error recovery (single profile failure doesnt crash loop)

### 6. Profile State Transitions
```
Unknown -> Checking -> Ready
Unknown -> Checking -> Expired -> Refreshing -> Ready
Unknown -> Checking -> Expired -> Refreshing -> Error
Ready -> Expired (TTL passed)
Refreshing -> Ready (success)
Refreshing -> Error (failure)
Error -> Refreshing (retry)
```

## Logging Requirements
Each test MUST log:
- Initial state
- Action being taken
- State after action
- Time taken for operations
- Any errors with full context

## Mock Components
```go
type MockRefresher struct {
    refreshDelay time.Duration
    shouldFail   bool
    callCount    int
}

func (m *MockRefresher) Refresh(ctx context.Context, profile string) error {
    m.callCount++
    time.Sleep(m.refreshDelay)
    if m.shouldFail {
        return errors.New("mock refresh failure")
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All state transitions tested
- [ ] Concurrent access is safe (passes -race)
- [ ] Monitor loop is tested
- [ ] Mock refresher allows failure injection
- [ ] 90%+ code coverage

## Files to Create
- internal/authpool/pool_test.go
- internal/authpool/monitor_test.go
- internal/authpool/mock_test.go

 {"id":"caam-xa9s","title":"TUI: Wire activate action to vault.Activate() with confirmation dialog","description":"Wire handleActivateProfile() (model.go:802-812) to call vault.Activate().

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. In handleActivateProfile(), don't immediately activate
2. Set state to stateConfirm with new confirmActivate action
3. On confirm, call vault.Activate(fileSet, profileName)
4. Log event to DB
5. Refresh profile list
6. Show success/error in statusMsg

WHY CONFIRMATION: Unlike delete (which removes data), activate replaces current auth. If current auth isn't backed up, it's lost. Confirmation reminds user.

EDGE CASES:
- Profile doesn't exist in vault
- Auth file write permission denied
- Current auth already matches target (no-op)

 {"id":"caam-xz27","title":"Create internal/logs package structure","description":"# Task: Create internal/logs package structure

## Overview
Create the foundational package structure for log file parsing. This package will be the home for all CLI log parsing functionality.

## Technical Details

### Package Location
`internal/logs/` (new package)

### Files to Create
1. `logs.go` - Common types and interfaces
2. `scanner.go` - Base scanner implementation
3. `claude.go` - Claude-specific log parsing
4. `codex.go` - Codex-specific log parsing
5. `types.go` - Shared data structures

### Core Interfaces
```go
// Scanner is the interface for provider-specific log scanners
type Scanner interface {
    // Scan parses logs from the given directory since the given time
    Scan(ctx context.Context, logDir string, since time.Time) (*ScanResult, error)
    
    // LogDir returns the default log directory for this provider
    LogDir() string
}

// ScanResult contains parsed log data
type ScanResult struct {
    Provider      string
    TotalEntries  int
    ParsedEntries int
    ParseErrors   int
    Since         time.Time
    Until         time.Time
    Entries       []*LogEntry
}
```

### Core Types
```go
// LogEntry represents a single log entry from any provider
type LogEntry struct {
    Timestamp       time.Time
    Type            string   // "request", "response", "error"
    Model           string   // "claude-3-opus", "gpt-4o", etc.
    ConversationID  string
    MessageID       string
    
    // Token counts
    InputTokens     int64
    OutputTokens    int64
    CacheReadTokens int64
    CacheCreateTokens int64
    TotalTokens     int64
    
    // Raw data for provider-specific fields
    Raw             map[string]any
}

// TokenUsage aggregates token counts
type TokenUsage struct {
    InputTokens       int64
    OutputTokens      int64  
    CacheReadTokens   int64
    CacheCreateTokens int64
    TotalTokens       int64
    
    ByModel map[string]*ModelTokenUsage
}

type ModelTokenUsage struct {
    Model       string
    InputTokens int64
    OutputTokens int64
    TotalTokens int64
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Package compiles with no errors
- [ ] All interfaces and types documented
- [ ] Scanner interface allows for provider-specific implementations
- [ ] LogEntry can represent entries from Claude, Codex, and Gemini

## Files Affected
- internal/logs/logs.go (new)
- internal/logs/scanner.go (new)
- internal/logs/types.go (new)

## Dependencies
None - this is foundational

## Blocks
- Claude log scanner implementation
- Codex log scanner implementation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:16:51.865542602-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:42:52.686823603-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:42:52.686823603-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented internal/logs package with Scanner interface, LogEntry, TokenUsage types, and MultiScanner. 21 tests passing."}
 {"id":"caam-y0ia","title":"[FEATURE] Enhanced Status with Cooldown TTL: Show cooldown remaining time in 'caam status' output","description":"BACKGROUND: Cooldown tracking exists (caam-5ed completed). 'caam cooldown list' shows active cooldowns, but 'caam status' doesn't surface this critical info. User has to run two commands to get the full picture.

CURRENT STATUS OUTPUT (root.go:237-331):
  Active Profiles
  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  claude      alice@gmail.com      🟢 healthy

PROPOSED ENHANCEMENT:
  claude      alice@gmail.com      🟢 healthy (cooldown: 47m remaining)
  codex       bob@gmail.com        🟡 warning: in cooldown (12m remaining)

ADDITIONAL ENHANCEMENT - ALL PROFILES IN COOLDOWN WARNING:
  claude: ⚠️ ALL profiles in cooldown (next available in 23m)

KEY FILES:
- cmd/caam/cmd/root.go lines 237-331 (statusCmd)
- internal/db/cooldown.go (ActiveCooldown, ListActiveCooldowns)

APPROACH:
1. In status command, after getting active profile, check cooldown
2. Calculate remaining time: cooldownUntil.Sub(time.Now())
3. Format as 'Xh Ym' or 'Xm' depending on duration
4. Add color coding: yellow for <30min, red for >1hr
5. Check if ALL profiles for provider are in cooldown, show warning

CONSIDERATIONS:
- What if profile is in cooldown but still active? This is valid - user hit limit, set cooldown, but didn't switch yet
- Should we show cooldown for non-active profiles? Not in basic status, maybe in 'status --verbose'
- Color coding should be optional (--no-color flag exists)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Status shows cooldown remaining time for active profile
- Warning when all profiles for a provider are in cooldown
- Color coding works
- --no-color still works

RATIONALE: Critical visibility. When you're about to use a profile, you need to know if it's in cooldown. Currently requires 'caam cooldown list' as separate step.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:19:42.829687919-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T20:53:57.980380976-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T20:53:57.980380976-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Both sub-tasks implemented - cooldown TTL display (caam-gy7z) and all-profiles-in-cooldown warning (caam-w5op)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-y0ia","depends_on_id":"caam-gy7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:27:32.721083332-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"caam-y0ia","depends_on_id":"caam-w5op","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T20:27:37.832533157-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"caam-y49","title":"Implement 'init' command for guided setup","description":"## Task
Add 'caam init' command for first-time setup.

## What it does
1. Creates data directories (~/.local/share/caam/{vault,profiles})
2. Creates default config file
3. Detects installed CLI tools
4. Optionally walks user through creating first profile

## Interactive Flow
```
$ caam init

Welcome to caam - Coding Agent Account Manager!

Creating directories...
  Created ~/.local/share/caam/vault
  Created ~/.local/share/caam/profiles

Detecting CLI tools...
  ✓ codex found
  ✓ claude found
  ✗ gemini not found

Would you like to create your first profile? [Y/n]
> y

Which tool? (codex/claude/gemini)
> claude

Profile name (e.g., work, personal, account-1)
> work

Created profile claude/work

Next steps:
  caam login claude work   # Authenticate
  caam backup claude work  # Save auth to vault
```

## Flags
- --quiet: Non-interactive, just create directories
- --force: Overwrite existing config

## Acceptance Criteria
- Creates necessary directories
- Detects installed tools
- Optional guided profile creation
- Idempotent (safe to run multiple times)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:55:08.611338867-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T01:40:02.199671201-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T01:40:02.199671201-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 'caam init' command for first-time setup: creates directories, detects CLI tools, shows next steps. Includes --quiet flag.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-y49","depends_on_id":"caam-1ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T00:55:08.625375856-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
